solution: 
```cpp
class Solution {
public:
    int findTheLongestSubstring(string s) {
        unordered_map<int, int> stateMap;
        stateMap[0] = -1;
        int mask = 0;
        int maxLen = 0;
        int vowels[26] = {0};
        vowels['a' - 'a'] = 1;
        vowels['e' - 'a'] = 2;
        vowels['i' - 'a'] = 4;
        vowels['o' - 'a'] = 8;
        vowels['u' - 'a'] = 16;

        for (int i = 0; i < s.size(); ++i) {
            if (vowels[s[i] - 'a'] != 0) {
                mask ^= vowels[s[i] - 'a'];
            }
            if (stateMap.count(mask)) {
                maxLen = max(maxLen, i - stateMap[mask]);
            } else {
                stateMap[mask] = i;
            }
        }
        return maxLen;
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a bitmask to track the parity (even/odd count) of vowels encountered so far. It maps each vowel to a unique bit in an integer mask. By XORing the mask when a vowel is encountered, we toggle the parity. If the same mask is seen again, it means the substring between the two indices has even counts of all vowels. The algorithm keeps track of the first occurrence of each mask and calculates the maximum length of valid substrings. This approach is efficient with linear time and constant space.
